mnemonics: PALS: Priority, Affinity, Load Balancing, Scheduling Algorithm
keyPoints: Thread scheduling decides which ready thread runs next on a CPU core.
Schedulers use algorithms like Round Robin, Priority-based, or Multi-level Feedback Queues.
The goal is to maximize CPU utilization and throughput while minimizing waiting time and response time.
commonMistakes: Confusing processes with threads; threads are lighter-weight units of execution within a process.
Assuming more threads always improve performance; excessive threads can lead to overhead and thrashing.
